title: ChatsDelta.destroyed strips Id back to String, forcing round-trip conversion
description:
ChangesResponse.destroyed is now Vec<Id> (correct, typed), but when the ChatsDelta AppEvent is constructed in chat_delta_sync, the ids are immediately mapped back to String:

  destroyed: changes.destroyed.into_iter().map(|id| id.to_string()).collect()

Then in app.rs apply_event, they are mapped back to &str:

  destroyed.iter().map(String::as_str).collect()

This round-trip (Id -> String -> &str) is unnecessary ceremony. The root cause is that event.rs ChatsDelta still declares destroyed: Vec<String>.

Rewrite: change event.rs ChatsDelta to:
  destroyed: Vec<jmap_chat::jmap::Id>,

Then the callsite in client_task.rs becomes:
  destroyed: changes.destroyed,

And app.rs becomes:
  destroyed.iter().map(|id| id.as_str()).collect()

This propagates the type discipline from the protocol layer all the way through to the UI layer.

Files: crates/jmap-chat-egui/src/event.rs (line 17), crates/jmap-chat-egui/src/client_task.rs (lines 1030-1034), crates/jmap-chat-egui/src/app.rs (lines 111-112).
close_reason: Resolved by 7p5.1: destroyed is now Vec<Id> throughout, no round-trip conversion
